decks.notes: The main concept of this Gruul Aggro deck is to quickly deploy aggressive creatures and burn spells to pressure the opponent and win the game as fast as possible. The deck focuses on dealing damage efficiently using creatures like Questing Druid, Monastery Swiftspear, and Emberheart Challenger, while also having access to burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage. The land base is optimized for speed and consistency, and cards like Sokenzan, Crucible of Defiance and Kumano Faces Kakkazan provide additional reach and finishers to close out games.

The deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo to overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards that support the combo like Bitter Triumph, Thoughtseize, and Cache Grab. The deck also includes various creatures and support spells to control the board and protect the combo pieces. It aims to aggressively play out its cards while disrupting the opponent's strategy, ultimately setting up for a powerful combo finish with Parhelion II. The deck is built around generating advantage through synergy between its cards and overwhelming the opponent with a powerful, game-winning combo.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to maintain control of the game through a combination of disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress, removal spells like Fatal Push and Go for the Throat, and powerful threats like Yorion, Sky Nomad and Narset, Parter of Veils. The deck also utilizes lands like Clearwater Pathway and Gloomlake Verge to fix mana and generate additional value. Overall, the strategy revolves around disrupting the opponent's game plan while deploying threats to close out the game efficiently.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly casting low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage to deal damage, backed up by spells like Reckless Rage and Torch the Tower for removal. Academic Dispute and Stormchaser's Talent provide card advantage and can help overwhelm opponents. Den of the Bugbear offers a potential late-game threat, while Experimental Synthesizer and Emberheart Challenger provide additional creatures to apply pressure. The deck aims to win by attacking aggressively and efficiently utilizing its spells to control the board and deal damage to the opponent.

Utilize spells and abilities to consistently return Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard to the battlefield quickly, while also controlling the board with removal spells and card draw. Maximize the use of instants and sorceries to trigger the Phoenix's ability, while also disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Picklock Prankster. Use Treasure Cruise to refill your hand and maintain pressure on the opponent. Adapt your game plan based on the matchup, prioritizing tempo and aggression to close out games swiftly.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming opponents with low-cost creatures like Novice Inspector and Ornithopter, backed up by efficient removal spells like Portable Hole and Gleeful Demolition. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Venerated Loxodon and Torch the Tower to close out games quickly. By applying constant pressure and disrupting the opponent's board state, this deck aims to establish dominance in the early game and maintain control until victory is achieved.

The main focus of this deck is to assemble and utilize the combo of Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes cards to support and protect the combo, with Monument to Endurance providing additional value. There are also removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck utilizes a mix of white and black mana sources, along with some utility lands to support consistency and flexibility in executing the combo.

This Gruul Aggro deck focuses on aggressive strategies to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It includes cards that deal direct damage like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, as well as creatures with haste like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast creatures and burn spells, utilizing cards like Soul-Scar Mage and Kumano Faces Kakkazan to maintain board presence and pressure. By utilizing the aggressive nature of the Gruul color combination, this deck aims to quickly close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.

This Boros Control deck focuses on utilizing a variety of cards to control the opponent's board while also generating value through creatures like Krenko's Buzzcrusher and Magmatic Hellkite. The deck also includes strong removal spells like Abrade and Cleansing Wildfire to deal with threats efficiently. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional card advantage and flexibility in longer games. The deck aims to establish board presence, disrupt the opponent's strategy, and ultimately outvalue them to secure victory.

The deck focuses on generating value through a combination of creature tokens, card advantage, and powerful combo pieces like Greasefang and Parhelion II. With access to removal, hand disruption, and ramp, the goal is to assemble the combo pieces while controlling the board and overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of tokens and powerful threats. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent while also having the potential for explosive turns and game-winning combos.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the interaction between Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that overwhelms the opponent. The deck includes cards like Concealed Courtyard and Blooming Marsh for mana acceleration, as well as Thoughtseize and Witherbloom Command for disruption. Boseiju, Who Endures provides protection for key spells, while Collective Brutality and Overlord of the Balemurk offer additional control options. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and go for the win before the opponent can react effectively.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively deal damage to the opponent using a combination of creatures, burn spells, and land activations. The deck aims to control the board with creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear while utilizing burn spells like Lightning Strike and Burst Lightning to deal direct damage to the opponent or to remove threats. Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional sources of damage, while cards like Screaming Nemesis and Monstrous Rage help to create a threatening board presence. Overall, the deck is built around the idea of quickly overwhelming the opponent with a flurry of aggressive plays.

This deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent. Utilizing cards like Monument to Endurance and Fleeting Spirit, it aims to control the board while setting up the combo pieces. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push provide disruption and removal, while cards like Guardian of New Benalia and Iron-Shield Elf offer protection and support. The deck also includes utility lands like Eiganjo, Seat of the Empire and Geier Reach Sanitarium to help in different situations. Ultimately, the key strategy is to efficiently set up the combo and outvalue the opponent.

The deck focuses on fast, aggressive plays with low-cost creatures and burn spells to quickly reduce the opponent's life total. Key cards include Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Burst Lightning to deal damage efficiently. Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional damage sources, while Reckless Rage offers removal options. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with constant pressure and burn spells to secure a quick victory before the opponent can stabilize. It prioritizes tempo and efficiency to maintain pressure and close out the game swiftly.

This Golgari Aggro deck focuses on early disruption and efficient creatures to pressure opponents quickly. Using cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the board while creatures like Badgermole Cub and Sentinel of the Nameless City provide aggressive threats. Mutavault and Ouroboroid offer versatility and resilience, with removal options like Abrupt Decay and Go for the Throat for further disruption. The deck aims to maintain tempo and apply constant pressure to overwhelm opponents before they have a chance to stabilize.

This deck focuses on using Greasefang Parhelion as a combo piece to generate powerful synergies with other cards like Iron-Shield Elf and Monument to Endurance. The goal is to control the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize while setting up the combo pieces for a devastating finish. The deck also includes various lands to support its color requirements and protect key creatures. Overall, the strategy revolves around assembling the right pieces at the right time to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.
